one_time_description: Germyn Sok Settlement with Canada for $75,000,000; subsequently fully allocated/disbursed to membership, resulting in net nil impact on annual surplus
economic_notes: Hailcistut Incremental House Post Agreement with Canada provides $37,771,963 over 3 years for self-government, housing and infrastructure, economic development, language, and fisheries. Additional DFO and provincial funding anticipated. COVID-19 declared pandemic March 11, 2020 with potential material impact on operations.
notes: Prior period restatement: Heiltsuk Kaxla Society and Heiltsuk College Society removed from consolidation as insufficient evidence of control; CMHC replacement reserve reclassification also restated 2019 figures. $75M Germyn Sok settlement received and fully allocated to membership (net nil to surplus). Treaty loan forgiveness of $3,964,296 recognized as revenue. 5 fully consolidated subsidiaries (Bella Bella Fuel Co., Bella Bella Band Store Ltd., Bella Bella Fisheries Ltd., Heiltsuk Environmental Services Ltd., Heiltsuk Section 95 Housing). 3 GBEs on modified equity basis (Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP, Heiltsuk Holdings LP, Heiltsuk Holdings Ltd.). No remuneration schedule included in the document. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC $14,202,999 + Province of BC $875,326 + DFO $2,854,259 + CMHC $175,719 + Dept of Justice Canada $62,916 + Great Bear Initiative $572,583 = $18,743,802; BC FN Gaming Revenue Share LP $1,088,557 added as government transfer for total $19,832,359 — figure used is sum of identifiable government sources excluding GBI ambiguity; long_term_debt = loans payable $2,888,593 + CMHC mortgages $1,931,025 = $4,819,618; annual_debt_service = loan repayments $88,971 + $1,036,114 proceeds netted but principal repayments on CMHC $243,490 + loans $88,971 = $332,461.
